step1.title: Create an account
step1.subtitle: We'll set up your profile, track and walk you through the first task. Under 3 minutes.

step2.title: What do you write?
step2.subtitle: Pick up to 3 languages — we'll tailor tasks and mock sessions to your stack.

step3.title: Let's solve the first task together
step3.subtitle: No rush: from problem to submit, with a tour of the interface and shortcuts.
step3.f1_sub: We'll cover every UI block — nothing skipped
step3.f2_sub: Tests first, submit second — like real engineers
step3.f3_sub: First task — no timer, sandbox mode
step3.task_text: Given an array of numbers and a target. Return indexes of two numbers that sum to target. Each number used once.
step3.tooltip_sub: Don't worry — nobody sees your fails
step3.title_prefix: Where shall we start ramping up
step3.subtitle_pick: Pick one core node. It becomes your active point on the Atlas.

two-pointers.title: Two pointers
two-pointers.blurb: The simplest way to feel an array. 80% of sliding-window problems become trivial after.

sliding-window.title: Sliding window
sliding-window.blurb: Base pattern for substrings/subarrays with dynamic bounds.

binary-search.title: Binary search
binary-search.blurb: Not only on arrays — on answers, functions, and boundary problems.

http-deep.title: HTTP deep
http-deep.blurb: Statuses, headers, idempotency, redirects, caching — interview-grade knowledge.

caching-strategies.title: Cache strategies
caching-strategies.blurb: read-through / write-back / write-around — what to pick and what you pay for it.

api-design.title: API design
api-design.blurb: Versioning, contract evolution, REST vs gRPC trade-offs.

cap-theorem.title: CAP & consistency
cap-theorem.blurb: Consistency ↔ availability under partition. Why single-master is not magic.

load-balancing.title: Load balancing
load-balancing.blurb: L4 vs L7, sticky sessions, health-checks, hashing strategies.

sharding.title: Sharding & partitioning
sharding.blurb: Range / hash / directory — when each and how to migrate.

locks-mutex.title: Locks & mutex
locks-mutex.blurb: Mutex vs RWLock, deadlock, lock ordering, lock-free hints.

channels-csp.title: Channels / CSP
channels-csp.blurb: Go-style: share by communicating, fan-in/fan-out, cancellation.

race-conditions.title: Race conditions
race-conditions.blurb: race detector, memory barriers, happens-before — so you don't bluff at the interview.

sql-windows.title: SQL window functions
sql-windows.blurb: ROW_NUMBER / LAG / LEAD / partitioned aggregates — must for analysts.

ab-testing.title: A/B testing
ab-testing.blurb: CUPED, SRM, multiple comparisons — what they ask product analysts.

probability.title: Probability basics
probability.blurb: Bayes, conditional, expectation — hygiene for DS interviews.

step4.title: Master your first pattern with AI-coach
step4.subtitle: Coach watches how you solve, hints in the moment, explains mistakes. Not graded — practice.
step4.ready_sub: After the session the pattern lands in your Codex and strict-mode mock unlocks.

step1_welcome.body: Strict mock with watermark, AI-coach with memory, skill map. For those with a base who need an objective readiness check.

goal_wizard.quick_or_build: Or build the goal from scratch below.

field.custom_placeholder: Example: SDET in fintech with Python + automation tests
field.date_hint: Without a date AI plans "when ready". A date sets the tempo — F3 predictions will count weeks to ready.

top_tier_co.label: Senior at Top-Tier Co
top_tier_co.hint: Google · Yandex · Wildberries · Ozon · Tinkoff · VK · Meta · Amazon

any_senior.label: Senior at any Co
any_senior.hint: Generic senior IT track — no specific employer

ml_offer.label: ML Engineer offer
ml_offer.hint: ML / DS role in any company

custom.label: Custom
custom.hint: Describe the goal in free text — AI will parse later

quiz.change_track_tooltip: Change track — current answers will be reset
quiz.subhead: 8 questions · ~2 minutes. You'll get 3 first actions + a suggested goal.
quiz.all_answered_pre: All 8 are already answered — you can jump straight to
quiz.picker_subtitle: 8 questions tailored to your track. You can change track later (answers will reset).
quiz.done_subtitle: 3 actions + a suggested goal. Pick any — the coach will remember.
quiz.proposed_goal_hint: The coach will remember this goal in every chat and tailor its recommendations. You can edit it via GoalWizard (the «Goal» card in Coach memory).
